column9: New WTP/Well (Development of the Ambrose Well and the construction of a Water Treatment Plant to offset the potential loss of the Birchfield WTP.) Planning and Design
WTP Expansion (Expansion of the existing Elka WTP from 500 GPD to 1,000 GPD to include the construction of a new well and plant controls.)
WTP Improvements (Development of needed improvements to the Birchfield WTP to recover the source water, resolve turbidity issues, and return the plant to operations.) Planning and Design

column7: First Utility District of Carter County*
column9: GREEN -Waterline Replacement (Replacement of approximately 20,500 linear feet of 2inch thru 6-inch diameter waterlines and service lines along Hodge Branch Road, Pearl Bowers Road, Harrell Road, Dawn Drive, South Street, Carl Taylor Road, Wilson Pit/Blue Springs, Bill Nave Road, and Willow Springs Road.) Energy Efficiency

WTP improvements (Improvements to the raw water intake, flocculation and sedimentation process, filtration, and SCADA; and the addition of a clearwell, granular activated carbon contactors, chemical storage and feed systems; and electrical upgrades.) Construction

GREEN - Waterline Replacement (Installation of approximately 53,600 linear feet of 8-inch and 12-inch diameter ductile iron and high-density polyethylene waterlines and associated appurtenances; and the abandonment of approximately 26,800 linear feet of existing water main. )
Planning and Design

GREEN - Distribution System Improvements (Installation of approximately 26,500 linear feet of 8-inch and 12-inch diameter ductile iron waterlines and 300 linear feet of 12-inch high-density polyethylene waterlines; and associated appurtenances.)
Water Efficiency

WTP Improvements (Conduct a pilot study to determine filter media and filtration system needed to remove Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) from source water at the Howard C. Brown Water Treatment Plant.) Planning and Design
GREEN- Water Meter Replacement (Replacement of approximately 3,600 water meters throughout the distribution system.) Water Efficiency
Water Storage Tank Rehabilitation (Rehabilitation of the Martin Creek, McNabb, Obrien, and Murphy/Birchfield Water Storage Tanks.)

WTP Expansion (Expanding the existing WTP from 4 MGD to 6 MGD to include the construction of settling and flocculation basins, a flash mix facility, a filter, storage, and electrical building; installation of new piping, valves, actuators, instrumentation and controls, and an electromagnetic flow meter; and the demolition of the old 2 MGD facility.)

GREEN- WTP Improvements (Improvements to include replacing existing filter beds with membrane technology, rehabilitation of baffles in the clear well, conversion of chlorine gas to sodium hypochlorite, installation of new pressure monitors, and updates to the operations building to remove Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) and perfluorooctane sulfonic acid (PFOS) contaminants.) Environmentally Innovative
WTP Improvements (Construction of new parallel treatment equipment; replacement of tube settlers, sludge collection and flocculation equipment; update the SCADA system; and well pump house upgrades.)

Distribution System Improvements (Rehabilitation of the pump station along John B Dennis Road; and the replacement of approximately 13,300 linear feet of 6-inch polyvinyl chloride waterlines along Morelock Street and Old Beason Well Road.)

WTP Upgrade (Construction of a granular activated carbon treatment facility consisting of ten carbon contactors and associated piping for the removal of Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) and perfluorooctane sulfonic acid (PFOS) from the Citico WTP.)
Distribution System Improvements (Construction of a new well at the end of Hill Road, and the installation of a 2-inch diameter waterline to the existing water treatment plant.)
Water Transmission Line Replacement (Installation of approximately 26,000 linear feet of new transmission lines from the WTP to the Hwy 92 and Hwy 11E Water Storage Tanks.) Planning and Design
Waterline Extension (Installation of approximately 69,000 linear feet of 24-inch diameter ductile iron pipe from Douglas Lake to the Pigeon Forge Water Treatment Facility.) Construction

column9: Waterline Replacement (Replacement of approximately 18,100 linear feet of 6-inch and 12-inch diameter ductile iron waterline with high-density polyethylene and polyvinyl chloride pipe along North Mohawk Road, Black Road, Old Knoxville Highway, and Twin Barns Road.)
